Implements functions for drawing various types of menu cells in the Pebble firmware UI.

# Purpose
The code is a C source file that provides functionality for drawing menu cells in a graphical user interface. It defines several functions and structures to manage the dimensions and appearance of menu cells, including basic cells, title cells, and header cells. The code includes a `MenuCellDimensions` structure to store the dimensions for different content sizes, and it provides functions to retrieve these dimensions based on the runtime platform's default size. The file also includes functions to draw various types of menu cells, such as [`menu_cell_basic_draw`](<#menu_cell_basic_draw>), [`menu_cell_title_draw`](<#menu_cell_title_draw>), and [`menu_cell_basic_header_draw`](<#menu_cell_basic_header_draw>), which handle the rendering of text and icons within the cells.

The code imports several headers, indicating its reliance on external libraries for graphics and UI components, such as `graphics.h` and `kino_reel.h`. It uses functions like `graphics_draw_text` and `graphics_draw_bitmap_in_rect` to render text and images. The code also includes logic to handle different content sizes and alignments, ensuring that the menu cells are drawn correctly on different devices and screen sizes. The use of static inline functions, such as [`prv_draw_icon`](<#prv_draw_icon>) and [`prv_menu_cell_basic_draw_custom_rect`](<#prv_menu_cell_basic_draw_custom_rect>), helps optimize performance by reducing function call overhead. Overall, the code is part of a larger system for managing and displaying menu interfaces in an application.
# Imports and Dependencies

---
- `menu_layer.h`
- `applib/graphics/graphics.h`
- `applib/ui/kino/kino_reel.h`
- `applib/ui/kino/kino_reel_gbitmap_private.h`
- `process_management/process_manager.h`
- `shell/system_theme.h`
- `system/passert.h`
- `util/math.h`


# Global Variables

---
### s\_menu\_cell\_dimensions
- **Type**: ``MenuCellDimensions` array`
- **Description**: Defines an array of `MenuCellDimensions` structures for different preferred content sizes. Each element in the array specifies dimensions such as `basic_cell_height`, `small_cell_height`, `horizontal_inset`, and `title_subtitle_left_margin` for menu cells.
- **Use**: Used to provide specific dimensions for menu cells based on the preferred content size.


# Data Structures

---
### MenuCellDimensions
- **Type**: ``struct``
- **Members**:
    - ``basic_cell_height``: Height of a basic menu cell.
    - ``small_cell_height``: Height of a small menu cell.
    - ``horizontal_inset``: Horizontal inset for menu cell content.
    - ``title_subtitle_left_margin``: Left margin for title and subtitle in a menu cell.
- **Description**: Defines the dimensions and layout properties for different types of menu cells, including basic and small cell heights, horizontal insets, and margins for titles and subtitles.

### prv\_get\_dimensions\_for\_runtime\_platform\_default\_size<!-- {{#callable:prv_get_dimensions_for_runtime_platform_default_size}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/applib/ui/menu_layer_system_cells.c#L72>)

Retrieves the menu cell dimensions for the default content size of the runtime platform.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Calls `system_theme_get_default_content_size_for_runtime_platform()` to get the default content size for the runtime platform.
    - Uses the returned `PreferredContentSize` to index into the `s_menu_cell_dimensions` array.
    - Returns a pointer to the `MenuCellDimensions` structure corresponding to the default content size.
- **Output**: A pointer to a `MenuCellDimensions` structure for the default content size of the runtime platform.

### prv\_get\_cell\_title\_font<!-- {{#callable:prv_get_cell_title_font}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/applib/ui/menu_layer_system_cells.c#L94>)

Retrieves the title font for a menu cell, defaulting to a system font if not specified.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``config``: A pointer to a `MenuCellLayerConfig` structure that contains configuration data for the menu cell, including the title font.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Check if `config->title_font` is not null.
    - If `config->title_font` is not null, return it.
    - If `config->title_font` is null, call `system_theme_get_font_for_default_size` with `TextStyleFont_MenuCellTitle` and return the result.
- **Output**: Returns a `GFont` object representing the font to use for the menu cell title.

- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Check if `config->subtitle_font` is not NULL.
    - If `config->subtitle_font` is not NULL, return it.
    - If `config->subtitle_font` is NULL, call `system_theme_get_font_for_default_size` with `TextStyleFont_MenuCellSubtitle` and return the result.
- **Output**: Returns a `GFont` object representing the subtitle font for the menu cell.

- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Check if `config->value_font` is not null.
    - If `config->value_font` is not null, return it.
    - If `config->value_font` is null, call `prv_get_cell_title_font(config)` and return its result.

### prv\_draw\_icon<!-- {{#callable:prv_draw_icon}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/applib/ui/menu_layer_system_cells.c#L107>)

Draws an icon on a graphics context with optional tinting based on legacy mode and icon format.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``ctx``: A pointer to the `GContext` where the icon will be drawn.
    - ``icon``: A pointer to the `GBitmap` representing the icon to draw.
    - ``icon_frame``: A pointer to the `GRect` defining the frame in which to draw the icon.
    - ``is_legacy2``: A boolean indicating if the legacy mode is active.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Check if `is_legacy2` is false to determine if compositing mode adjustments are needed.
    - If `icon` is not null and its format is `GBitmapFormat1Bit`, set the compositing mode to `GCompOpTint`.
    - If the current compositing mode is `GCompOpAssign`, set it to `GCompOpSet`.
    - Draw the `icon` in the specified `icon_frame` on the `ctx`.
- **Output**: No return value; the function modifies the graphics context `ctx` to draw the icon.

### prv\_menu\_cell\_basic\_draw\_custom\_rect<!-- {{#callable:prv_menu_cell_basic_draw_custom_rect}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/applib/ui/menu_layer_system_cells.c#L121>)

Draws a custom rectangular menu cell with optional icon, title, subtitle, and value text, adjusting layout based on configuration and legacy support.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``ctx``: A pointer to the `GContext` used for drawing operations.
    - ``cell_layer``: A pointer to the `Layer` representing the menu cell to be drawn.
    - ``config``: A pointer to the `MenuCellLayerConfig` structure containing configuration details for the cell, such as fonts, text, icon, and alignment.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Get the bounds of the `cell_layer` and determine if the legacy SDK is used.
    - Retrieve fonts for the title and subtitle, and calculate their heights.
    - Calculate the full height of the cell content and determine horizontal and vertical margins.
    - If an icon is present, calculate its position and draw it using [`prv_draw_icon`](<#prv_draw_icon>).
    - Adjust the drawing box based on icon alignment and presence.
    - Calculate the position and size of the value box, adjusting for overflow mode.
    - Set text color to black if using legacy SDK.
    - Draw the value text if present and not aligned to the right.
    - Draw the title text if present.
    - Draw the subtitle text if present, adjusting its position based on the title height.
- **Output**: No return value; the function performs drawing operations directly on the provided `GContext`.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`prv_get_cell_title_font`](<#prv_get_cell_title_font>)

### prv\_grect\_inset<!-- {{#callable:prv_grect_inset}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/applib/ui/menu_layer_system_cells.c#L227>)

Adjusts a [`GRect`](<../graphics/gtypes.h.md#GRect>) by insetting it with specified `GEdgeInsets`, ensuring the resulting rectangle is valid.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``rect``: A pointer to a [`GRect`](<../graphics/gtypes.h.md#GRect>) structure that will be modified by the insets.
    - ``insets``: A pointer to a `GEdgeInsets` structure that specifies the insets to apply to the rectangle.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Standardizes the `rect` using [`grect_standardize`](<../graphics/gtypes.c.md#grect_standardize>) to ensure it is in a normalized form.
    - Calculates the new width by subtracting the left and right insets from the rectangle's width.
    - Calculates the new height by subtracting the top and bottom insets from the rectangle's height.
    - Checks if the new width or height is less than zero; if so, sets `rect` to `GRectZero`.
    - If the new dimensions are valid, updates `rect` with the new origin and size based on the insets.
- **Output**: Modifies the `rect` in place to reflect the insets, or sets it to `GRectZero` if the insets make it invalid.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`grect_standardize`](<../graphics/gtypes.c.md#grect_standardize>)
    - [`GRect`](<../graphics/gtypes.h.md#GRect>)

### prv\_should\_render\_subtitle\_round<!-- {{#callable:prv_should_render_subtitle_round}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/applib/ui/menu_layer_system_cells.c#L239>)

Determines if a subtitle should be rendered for a menu cell based on its selection state and configuration.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``config``: A pointer to a `MenuCellLayerConfig` structure that contains configuration details for the menu cell, including the subtitle and value text.
    - ``is_selected``: A boolean indicating whether the menu cell is currently selected.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Checks if the cell is selected or if there is a value text present in the `config`.
    - Verifies that the `subtitle` in the `config` is not `NULL`.
    - Returns `true` if either the cell is selected or there is a value text, and the subtitle is not `NULL`.
- **Output**: Returns a boolean value indicating whether the subtitle should be rendered (`true`) or not (`false`).

### prv\_menu\_cell\_basic\_draw\_custom\_one\_column\_round<!-- {{#callable:prv_menu_cell_basic_draw_custom_one_column_round}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/applib/ui/menu_layer_system_cells.c#L245>)

Draws a custom one-column round menu cell with title, subtitle, and icon alignment based on configuration and available space.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``ctx``: A pointer to the `GContext` used for drawing operations.
    - ``cell_layer_bounds``: A pointer to the `GRect` defining the bounds of the cell layer.
    - ``config``: A pointer to the `MenuCellLayerConfig` structure containing configuration for the cell, including fonts, text, and icon.
    - ``text_alignment``: The `GTextAlignment` value specifying the alignment of the text within the cell.
    - ``container_alignment``: The `GAlign` value specifying the alignment of the container within the cell.
    - ``is_selected``: A boolean indicating whether the cell is selected.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Check if `cell_layer_bounds` is NULL and return `GRectZero` if true.
    - Get the title font and its height, and check if it fits within the cell height; return `GRectZero` if it does not.
    - Initialize subtitle text height and icon frame size to zero.
    - Determine if the subtitle and icon should be rendered based on configuration and selection status.
    - Calculate the title text frame height based on whether multiple lines can be used and the overflow mode.
    - Adjust container height based on the presence and alignment of the icon and subtitle.
    - Align the container rectangle within the cell bounds using the specified alignment.
    - Draw the icon if it fits within the cell and is configured to be rendered.
    - Adjust the position of the title and subtitle based on the icon's position and alignment.
    - Draw the subtitle if it is configured to be rendered and fits within the cell.
    - Draw the title, ensuring it fits within the available space, and return the rectangle used for the title.
- **Output**: Returns a `GRect` representing the area used for the title text frame.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`prv_get_cell_title_font`](<#prv_get_cell_title_font>)
    - [`prv_get_cell_subtitle_font`](<#prv_get_cell_subtitle_font>)
    - [`prv_should_render_subtitle_round`](<#prv_should_render_subtitle_round>)
    - [`gsize_add`](<../graphics/gtypes.h.md#gsize_add>)
    - [`graphics_text_layout_get_text_height`](<../graphics/text_layout.c.md#graphics_text_layout_get_text_height>)
    - [`GSize`](<../graphics/gtypes.h.md#GSize>)
    - [`grect_align`](<../graphics/gtypes.c.md#grect_align>)
    - [`gsize_equal`](<../graphics/gtypes.c.md#gsize_equal>)
    - [`gpoint_add`](<../graphics/gtypes.h.md#gpoint_add>)
    - [`prv_draw_icon`](<#prv_draw_icon>)
    - [`grect_get_max_y`](<../graphics/gtypes.h.md#grect_get_max_y>)
    - [`grect_get_max_x`](<../graphics/gtypes.h.md#grect_get_max_x>)

### prv\_menu\_cell\_basic\_draw\_custom\_two\_columns\_round<!-- {{#callable:prv_menu_cell_basic_draw_custom_two_columns_round}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/applib/ui/menu_layer_system_cells.c#L442>)

Draws a menu cell with two columns, handling text and icon placement based on configuration.
- **Inputs**:
    - `ctx`: A pointer to the `GContext` used for drawing operations.
    - `cell_layer_bounds`: A pointer to the `GRect` defining the bounds of the cell layer.
    - `config`: A pointer to the `MenuCellLayerConfig` structure containing configuration for the cell, such as fonts, text, and icons.
    - `is_selected`: A boolean indicating whether the cell is selected.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Check if `cell_layer_bounds` is NULL and return if true.
    - Determine the size of the icon if present, otherwise use `GSizeZero`.
    - Calculate the size of the right element (either value text or icon) using `graphics_text_layout_get_max_used_size` for text or icon size.
    - Inset the left column's rectangle by the width of the right element using [`prv_grect_inset`](<#prv_grect_inset>).
    - Draw the left column content using [`prv_menu_cell_basic_draw_custom_one_column_round`](<#prv_menu_cell_basic_draw_custom_one_column_round>).
    - If the left column content could not be drawn, return.
    - Set the rectangle for the right element based on the maximum x-coordinate of the left column and its size.
    - If a value is present, adjust the y-origin by the font cap offset and draw the text using `graphics_draw_text`.
    - If no value is present, align and clip the icon rectangle, and draw the icon if it fits.
- **Output**: No return value; the function performs drawing operations on the provided `GContext`.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`prv_get_cell_value_font`](<#prv_get_cell_value_font>)
    - [`prv_grect_inset`](<#prv_grect_inset>)
    - [`GEdgeInsets`](<../graphics/gtypes.h.md#GEdgeInsets>)
    - [`prv_menu_cell_basic_draw_custom_one_column_round`](<#prv_menu_cell_basic_draw_custom_one_column_round>)
    - [`grect_equal`](<../graphics/gtypes.c.md#grect_equal>)
    - [`GPoint`](<../graphics/gtypes.h.md#GPoint>)
    - [`grect_get_max_x`](<../graphics/gtypes.h.md#grect_get_max_x>)
    - [`grect_clip`](<../graphics/gtypes.c.md#grect_clip>)
    - [`grect_align`](<../graphics/gtypes.c.md#grect_align>)
    - [`gsize_equal`](<../graphics/gtypes.c.md#gsize_equal>)
    - [`gpoint_add`](<../graphics/gtypes.h.md#gpoint_add>)
    - [`prv_draw_icon`](<#prv_draw_icon>)

### prv\_menu\_cell\_basic\_draw\_custom\_round<!-- {{#callable:prv_menu_cell_basic_draw_custom_round}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/applib/ui/menu_layer_system_cells.c#L500>)

Draws a custom round menu cell with either one or two columns based on the configuration.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``ctx``: A pointer to the `GContext` used for drawing operations.
    - ``cell_layer``: A pointer to the `Layer` representing the menu cell to be drawn.
    - ``config``: A pointer to the `MenuCellLayerConfig` structure containing configuration details for the cell.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Check if the cell is highlighted using `menu_cell_layer_is_highlighted` to determine if it is selected.
    - Determine if two columns should be drawn based on the presence of a value or an icon aligned to the right.
    - Calculate the horizontal inset based on whether the cell is selected and if two columns are drawn.
    - Adjust the cell layer bounds using [`prv_grect_inset`](<#prv_grect_inset>) with the calculated insets.
    - If two columns are needed, call [`prv_menu_cell_basic_draw_custom_two_columns_round`](<#prv_menu_cell_basic_draw_custom_two_columns_round>) to draw the cell with two columns.
    - If only one column is needed, call [`prv_menu_cell_basic_draw_custom_one_column_round`](<#prv_menu_cell_basic_draw_custom_one_column_round>) to draw the cell with one column, centered.
- **Output**: No direct output; the function performs drawing operations on the provided `GContext`.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`prv_grect_inset`](<#prv_grect_inset>)
    - [`GEdgeInsets`](<../graphics/gtypes.h.md#GEdgeInsets>)
    - [`prv_menu_cell_basic_draw_custom_two_columns_round`](<#prv_menu_cell_basic_draw_custom_two_columns_round>)
    - [`prv_menu_cell_basic_draw_custom_one_column_round`](<#prv_menu_cell_basic_draw_custom_one_column_round>)

### prv\_draw\_cell<!-- {{#callable:prv_draw_cell}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/applib/ui/menu_layer_system_cells.c#L525>)

Selects and calls the appropriate function to draw a menu cell based on the platform's screen shape.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``ctx``: A pointer to the `GContext` object used for drawing operations.
    - ``cell_layer``: A pointer to the `Layer` object representing the cell to be drawn.
    - ``config``: A pointer to the `MenuCellLayerConfig` object containing configuration details for the cell.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Uses the `PBL_IF_RECT_ELSE` macro to determine if the platform is rectangular or round.
    - Calls `prv_menu_cell_basic_draw_custom_rect` if the platform is rectangular.
    - Calls `prv_menu_cell_basic_draw_custom_round` if the platform is round.
- **Output**: No return value; the function performs drawing operations directly on the provided `GContext`.

[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/applib/ui/menu_layer_system_cells.c#L532>)

- **Inputs**:

    - ``cell_layer``: A pointer to the `Layer` representing the cell to be drawn.

- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Calls the [`prv_draw_cell`](<#prv_draw_cell>) function with the provided `ctx`, `cell_layer`, and `config` arguments.
    - The [`prv_draw_cell`](<#prv_draw_cell>) function determines the appropriate drawing function based on the platform and calls it to render the cell.
- **Output**: No return value; the function performs drawing operations on the provided graphics context.

### prv\_draw\_basic<!-- {{#callable:prv_draw_basic}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/applib/ui/menu_layer_system_cells.c#L537>)

Configures and draws a basic menu cell with specified fonts, text, icon, and alignment.
- **Inputs**:
    - `ctx`: A pointer to the `GContext` used for drawing operations.
    - `cell_layer`: A pointer to the `Layer` representing the cell to draw.
    - `title_font`: The `GFont` to use for the title text.
    - `title`: A pointer to the title text string.
    - `value_font`: The `GFont` to use for the value text.
    - `value`: A pointer to the value text string.
    - `subtitle_font`: The `GFont` to use for the subtitle text.
    - `subtitle`: A pointer to the subtitle text string.
    - `icon`: A pointer to the `GBitmap` representing the icon to display.
    - `icon_on_right`: A boolean indicating if the icon should be aligned to the right.
    - `overflow_mode`: The `GTextOverflowMode` to use for text overflow handling.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Creates a `MenuCellLayerConfig` structure and initializes it with the provided fonts, text, icon, and alignment settings.
    - Determines the icon alignment based on the `icon_on_right` flag and the platform's shape (rectangular or round).
    - Calls the [`prv_draw_cell`](<#prv_draw_cell>) function with the drawing context, cell layer, and configuration to render the cell.
- **Output**: No return value; the function performs drawing operations on the provided `GContext`.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`prv_draw_cell`](<#prv_draw_cell>)

### menu\_cell\_basic\_draw\_custom<!-- {{#callable:menu_cell_basic_draw_custom}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/applib/ui/menu_layer_system_cells.c#L557>)

Draws a custom menu cell with specified fonts, text, icon, and layout options.
- **Inputs**:
    - `ctx`: A pointer to the `GContext` used for drawing operations.
    - `cell_layer`: A pointer to the `Layer` representing the cell to draw.
    - `title_font`: The `GFont` to use for the title text.
    - `title`: A pointer to the title text string.
    - `value_font`: The `GFont` to use for the value text.
    - `value`: A pointer to the value text string.
    - `subtitle_font`: The `GFont` to use for the subtitle text.
    - `subtitle`: A pointer to the subtitle text string.
    - `icon`: A pointer to a `GBitmap` representing the icon to display.
    - `icon_on_right`: A boolean indicating if the icon should be drawn on the right side.
    - `overflow_mode`: The `GTextOverflowMode` to use for text overflow handling.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Calls the [`prv_draw_basic`](<#prv_draw_basic>) function with the provided parameters to perform the actual drawing of the menu cell.
- **Output**: No return value; the function performs drawing operations on the provided `GContext`.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`prv_draw_basic`](<#prv_draw_basic>)


---
### menu\_cell\_basic\_draw\_icon\_right<!-- {{#callable:menu_cell_basic_draw_icon_right}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/applib/ui/menu_layer_system_cells.c#L565>)

Draws a basic menu cell with a title, subtitle, and an icon aligned to the right.
- **Inputs**:
    - `ctx`: A pointer to the `GContext` used for drawing operations.
    - `cell_layer`: A pointer to the `Layer` representing the cell to draw on.
    - `title`: A string representing the title text of the menu cell.
    - `subtitle`: A string representing the subtitle text of the menu cell.
    - `icon`: A pointer to a `GBitmap` representing the icon to display on the right side of the cell.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Calls the [`prv_draw_basic`](<#prv_draw_basic>) function with the provided context, cell layer, title, subtitle, and icon.
    - Sets the `icon_on_right` parameter to `true` to align the icon to the right.
    - Uses `GTextOverflowModeFill` for the text overflow mode.
- **Output**: No return value; the function performs drawing operations on the provided `GContext`.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`prv_draw_basic`](<#prv_draw_basic>)


---
### menu\_cell\_basic\_draw<!-- {{#callable:menu_cell_basic_draw}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/applib/ui/menu_layer_system_cells.c#L571>)

Draws a basic menu cell with a title, optional subtitle, and optional icon.
- **Inputs**:
    - `ctx`: A pointer to the `GContext` used for drawing operations.
    - `cell_layer`: A pointer to the `Layer` representing the cell to draw.
    - `title`: A string representing the title text of the cell.
    - `subtitle`: A string representing the subtitle text of the cell, or `NULL` if no subtitle is needed.
    - `icon`: A pointer to a `GBitmap` representing the icon to display, or `NULL` if no icon is needed.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Calls the [`prv_draw_basic`](<#prv_draw_basic>) function with the provided context, cell layer, title, subtitle, and icon.
    - Passes `NULL` for the title font, value font, and subtitle font, indicating default fonts should be used.
    - Sets `icon_on_right` to `false`, meaning the icon will be aligned to the left or top, depending on the platform.
    - Uses `GTextOverflowModeFill` for the text overflow mode, ensuring text fills the available space.
- **Output**: No return value; the function performs drawing operations directly on the provided `GContext`.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`prv_draw_basic`](<#prv_draw_basic>)


---
### menu\_cell\_title\_draw<!-- {{#callable:menu_cell_title_draw}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/applib/ui/menu_layer_system_cells.c#L580>)

Draws a title on a menu cell layer, adjusting for legacy SDKs if necessary.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``ctx``: A pointer to the `GContext` used for drawing operations.
    - ``cell_layer``: A pointer to the `Layer` representing the menu cell where the title will be drawn.
    - ``title``: A pointer to a string containing the title text to be drawn.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Checks if `title` is not null before proceeding.
    - Calls `process_manager_compiled_with_legacy2_sdk()` to determine if the legacy SDK is used.
    - If the legacy SDK is used, sets the text color to black using `graphics_context_set_text_color()`.
    - Retrieves the system font `FONT_KEY_GOTHIC_28` using `fonts_get_system_font()`.
    - Adjusts the `GRect` `box` to set the position and size for the text drawing area.
    - Draws the text using `graphics_draw_text()` with left alignment and fill overflow mode.
- **Output**: No return value; the function performs drawing operations on the provided `GContext`.


---
### menu\_cell\_basic\_header\_draw<!-- {{#callable:menu_cell_basic_header_draw}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/applib/ui/menu_layer_system_cells.c#L601>)

Draws a basic header cell with a title in a menu layer, adjusting text color for legacy apps.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``ctx``: A pointer to the `GContext` used for drawing operations.
    - ``cell_layer``: A pointer to the `Layer` representing the cell to draw on.
    - ``title``: A pointer to a string containing the title text to display.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Check if `title` is not NULL.
    - Determine if the app is compiled with the legacy 2.x SDK using `process_manager_compiled_with_legacy2_sdk()`.
    - If the app is legacy, set the text color to black using `graphics_context_set_text_color()`.
    - Get the system font `FONT_KEY_GOTHIC_14_BOLD` using `fonts_get_system_font()`.
    - Retrieve the bounds of `cell_layer` and adjust the origin for pixel nudging.
    - Draw the `title` text using `graphics_draw_text()` with left alignment and fill overflow mode.
- **Output**: No return value; the function performs drawing operations on the provided `GContext`.
